title: limnanthes gracilis var. parishii
intro: Limnanthes gracilis var. parishii, also known as Parish's meadowfoam, is a variety of flowering plant in the meadowfoam genus. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from the plant's seed oil. The seed oil of Parish's meadowfoam has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving skin health. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a salve or oil for topical application, and it can also be infused in oil or vinegar for internal use.
